questions.question: Explain the difference between strcmp() and strncmp() functions in C. Also write a C code snippet to compare two strings using strcmp() and display whether they are equal, greater, or less than each other.
keyPoints: strcmp() compares entire strings, strncmp() compares only first n characters
strcmp() returns 0 if equal, positive if s1 > s2, negative if s1 < s2
Comparison is case-sensitive based on ASCII values
strncmp() is safer when string lengths are unknown

questions.question: Write a C program to count the occurrences of a character in a string using strchr() or manual traversal. Explain your approach.
keyPoints: Use pointer arithmetic or strchr() to locate characters
Traverse string until null terminator is reached
Initialize counter to zero and increment for each match
Handle case sensitivity and edge cases (empty string)

questions.question: Write a C program that takes a sentence as input and performs the following operations: (a) Find the length of the entire sentence using strlen(), (b) Copy the sentence to another buffer using strcpy() or strncpy(), (c) Extract the first word using strtok(), (d) Check if a specific substring exists using strstr(), (e) Reverse the string without using built-in functions. Include proper validation and display all results.
keyPoints: Use strlen() for length, strcpy()/strncpy() for copying with buffer size check
strtok() tokenizes string using delimiters (space, punctuation)
strstr() returns pointer to first occurrence of substring or NULL if not found
Reverse string using two-pointer technique swapping characters

questions.question: (a) Write a function in C to remove all occurrences of a character from a string without using built-in functions. (b) Write another function to check if a string is a palindrome. (c) Implement a function to concatenate two strings manually (without using strcat()). Analyze the time and space complexity of each function.
keyPoints: Remove function: shift characters left when match found, maintain null terminator
Palindrome: use two pointers from both ends, compare characters
Concatenation: ensure destination has enough space, copy src to end of dest
Analyze O(n), O(n²) complexities based on implementation approach
